start.speaker: elara
start.text: Another stranger on the road. You've picked a poor time to visit Ashvale.

options.text: What's happening here? The fields look... dead.
[Show Silver Compass] Silas Maren. Does that name mean anything to you?
[Offer gold] You look like you haven't eaten properly in days.
Not my concern. I'm just passing through.
[Only if silo burned] The grain stores... I had a hand in that.

elara_recognition.speaker: elara
elara_recognition.text: Silas... that was my father's name. I haven't seen this compass since I was a child, before the night they took him to the manor. How did you— No, it doesn't matter. If you have this, then the forest sent you. Or the ghosts did.

elara_recognition_2.speaker: elara
elara_recognition_2.text: My father died for this soil, thirty years ago. He was the sacrifice that bought Ashvale its last 'peace.' Now the debt is due again, and the Lord just... ignores us. I won't let it happen twice. I'll find a way to stop this poison, or I'll burn the manor down myself.

village_explanation.speaker: elara
village_explanation.text: The crops started dying three weeks ago. Brother Maren says it's divine punishment for our sins. Lord Ashworth says nothing at all — he's locked himself in that manor of his.

village_explanation_2.speaker: elara
village_explanation_2.text: I'm an herbalist. I've been studying the dead soil. It's not blight. It's not drought. Something is wrong with the water.

options.text: The water? You think it's being poisoned deliberately?
Tell me about this Brother Maren. What does the Church want?
Why has the lord shut himself away?

poison_suspicion.speaker: elara
poison_suspicion.text: Keep your voice down. I... I found traces of nightcap root in the well water. That doesn't grow naturally here. Someone brought it in.

poison_suspicion_2.speaker: elara
poison_suspicion_2.text: I need someone to check the upstream water source in Thornwood. I can't go alone — the forest has been... hostile lately. Will you help me?

options.text: I'll investigate with you. When do we go?
I don't work for free. What's in it for me?
Sounds dangerous, and not my problem.

grateful_accept.speaker: elara
grateful_accept.text: Thank you. Meet me at the eastern edge of the village at dawn. And... be careful who you talk to about this. Not everyone wants the truth found.

payment_discussion.speaker: elara
payment_discussion.text: I... don't have much. But I know herbs that can heal wounds no potion can. And if we find the poisoner, Lord Ashworth will surely reward us. Please.

disappointed_response.speaker: elara
disappointed_response.text: I understand. The world teaches us to look out for ourselves. But people are dying here. If you change your mind, you know where to find me.

about_maren.speaker: elara
about_maren.text: Brother Maren arrived two months ago. Convenient timing, don't you think? He preaches that the crop death is the Ashen Light's judgment on our 'wickedness.' People are starting to believe him.

about_maren_2.speaker: elara
about_maren_2.text: Every day more villagers flock to his sermons. And every day, Lord Ashworth's authority weakens. I don't trust that man's piety.

about_lord.speaker: elara
about_lord.text: No one's seen Lord Ashworth in weeks. The guards say he's 'indisposed.' Greta at the inn says she's heard shouting from the manor at night. Something's eating at him.

grateful_response.speaker: elara
grateful_response.text: That's... that's very kind. Most travelers don't even look us in the eye these days. I'm Elara. If you need herbs or healing, find me at the edge of the market.

cold_response.speaker: elara
cold_response.text: Then pass through quickly. This village has enough ghosts already without adding another.

horrified_response.speaker: elara
horrified_response.text: You... YOU did this? Do you have any idea what you've done? Children will starve. Families will die. Get away from me. Get AWAY!
